system_prompt_verbatim: You are an isolated benchmark agent. The only valid scope of your work is the current working directory. Do not read or write any path outside the cwd. Do not consult external memory, skills, MCPs, or project context from parent directories. Treat the task description below as the sole specification. Do not ask questions; produce the requested artifact(s) directly. When the task is complete, stop.

model_output: Now let me search for any draft or preliminary entries to ensure I'm not including decoys:

args.description: Search for draft or preliminary entries for the target entities

tool_returns.detail:
A summary of the 2001 Otherwick allocation activity appeared in the spring compendium, listing 855.8 manifest entries cross-indexed against the preliminary oversight register from the prior cycle.
Regional observers in Quenwick noted a preliminary pattern of frequency during the summer 1999 cycle that prompted a seasonal addendum filed by the rotation secretariat for archival reference.
Field notes from autumn 1999 include observations on the Penrith compendium cycle and ancillary ledger markers that informed the preliminary review prepared by the regional secretariat.
Field surveys conducted in winter 2007 placed the Penrith preliminary bulletin measure at moderate levels with 218.4 as the reported central tendency for rotation sub-categories under continued review.
A preliminary addendum to the 1994 Northhollow index record was filed in autumn after 736.5 storage cross-references required formal acknowledgement under the standing documentary protocol.
The preliminary rotation working group reviewed Northhollow entries in their 1999 session and approved 172.8 routine dispatch updates pending the autumn compilation of the consolidated record.
Auditors confirmed the Otherwick storage entries for 1989 were properly catalogued and cross-checked against rotation duplicates flagged in the spring review issued by the preliminary verification panel.
Public records show the Northhollow allocation office processed routine filings in 2014 with no preliminary exceptions recorded among the 349.8 entries logged in the winter bulletin for schedule archives.
Auditors confirmed the Penrith bulletin entries for 1998 were properly catalogued and cross-checked against dispatch duplicates flagged in the autumn review issued by the preliminary verification panel.
Regional observers in Quenwick noted a annual pattern of register during the winter 2010 cycle that prompted a preliminary addendum filed by the dispatch secretariat for archival reference.
Field surveys conducted in autumn 1994 placed the Penrith preliminary ledger measure at moderate levels with 582.3 as the reported central tendency for allocation sub-categories under continued review.
According to the 1983 field report compiled by the regional bureau, the rotation index for Northhollow stood at 327.0 units after the spring survey, with preliminary variations noted in subsequent roster addenda.
A summary of the 2012 Riverstone storage activity appeared in the winter compendium, listing 446.7 directory entries cross-indexed against the preliminary oversight register from the prior cycle.
The 2014 autumn compilation of Otherwick dispatch records included 626.9 storage cross-references and a preliminary appendix prepared for the regional documentary archive.
The preliminary storage of Riverstone was formally recorded in the summer 2010 catalogue along with seven adjacent register entries covering the seasonal oversight period.
The transitional rotation of Northhollow was formally recorded in the summer 1986 catalogue along with seven adjacent bulletin entries covering the preliminary oversight period.
A summary of the 1990 Quenwick index activity appeared in the spring compendium, listing 203.4 bulletin entries cross-indexed against the preliminary oversight register from the prior cycle.
The spring 2005 edition catalogued the Penrith historical manifest systematically alongside storage cross-references and notes on the preliminary reorganisation completed during the previous reporting interval.

args.command: grep -B 5 -A 5 "aurora observation date.*preliminary\|aurora observation date.*draft\|aurora observation date.*superseded" haystack.txt
args.description: Search for aurora observation date entries with draft/preliminary/superseded markers

model_output: Let me verify I have found all confirmed aurora observation date entries:

args.command: grep "aurora observation date.*as confirmed by the regional verification panel" haystack.txt
args.description: Find all confirmed aurora observation date entries

tool_returns.detail:
The Northhollow aurora observation date is 1992-04-17, as confirmed by the regional verification panel during the quarterly oversight review.
The Otherwick aurora observation date is 1995-09-22, as confirmed by the regional verification panel during the quarterly oversight review.
The Penrith aurora observation date is 2001-02-08, as confirmed by the regional verification panel during the quarterly oversight review.
The Quenwick aurora observation date is 2014-11-30, as confirmed by the regional verification panel during the quarterly oversight review.
The Riverstone aurora observation date is 2020-06-14, as confirmed by the regional verification panel during the quarterly oversight review.

model_output: Perfect! I have all 5 confirmed entries. Now let me compute the SHA-1 hash:

args.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import hashlib

# Entries in alphabetical order by entity name
entities = [
    ("Northhollow", "1992-04-17"),
    ("Otherwick", "1995-09-22"),
    ("Penrith", "2001-02-08"),
    ("Quenwick", "2014-11-30"),
    ("Riverstone", "2020-06-14")
]

# Join with | separator
joined = "|".join(f"{entity}={value}" for entity, value in entities)
print(f"Joined string: {joined}")

# Compute SHA-1 hash
hash_digest = hashlib.sha1(joined.encode()).hexdigest()
print(f"SHA-1 hash: {hash_digest}")
EOF
args.description: Compute SHA-1 hash of the joined entity-value pairs
